topic: Comet Opik Review: Open-Source LLM Evaluation and Observability

key_points: Detailed head-to-head performance benchmarks between Langfuse, LangSmith, and Opik
Opik completed trace logging in 23.10 seconds with evaluation results available in 0.34 seconds
Recommends Langfuse for most teams, but Opik for teams already on Comet ML ecosystem
Highlights Opik's Apache 2.0 license as an advantage over more restrictive options
Developer deep dive focusing on practical setup experience with Opik
Covers tracing LLM calls, setting up LLM-as-a-judge evaluations, and using the UI
Highlights the one-line integration for agent frameworks
Positive review of the Prompt Playground feature for iterating on prompts
Positions Opik as a revolutionary platform for developer-focused LLM observability
Emphasizes Opik's 40M+ traces per day scalability milestone
Covers Agent Optimizer as a standout differentiator over competitors
Notes that Opik is trusted by 150,000+ developers globally
Investor/analyst perspective on Opik's position in the open-source LLM evals space
Discusses Comet's history in ML experiment tracking and how Opik extends that
Highlights rapid GitHub growth: 0 to 12,500 stars in ~8-9 months
Analyzes competitive positioning vs. Braintrust, Langfuse, and Arize
Comprehensive list of top open-source LLM observability tools
Lists Opik alongside Langfuse, Helicone, and Arize Phoenix
Focuses on self-hosting requirements and feature parity with paid tools
Notes Opik's Docker and Kubernetes deployment options for different team sizes

stats.fact: LLM observability platform market grew from $1.97B in 2025 to $2.69B in 2026 at a 36.3% CAGR
stats.source: Research and Markets, Large Language Model (LLM) Observability Platform Market Report 2026

stats.fact: The LLM observability market is projected to reach $9.26B by 2030 at a 36.2% CAGR
Gartner projects 60% of software engineering teams will adopt AI evaluation and observability platforms by 2028, up from just 18% in 2025
stats.source: Comet blog citing 2026 Gartner Market Guide for AI Evaluation and Observability Platforms

stats.fact: Comet was recognized in the 2026 Gartner Market Guide for AI Evaluation and Observability Platforms
Opik processes over 40 million traces daily across organizations from startups to Fortune 500 companies
stats.source: Comet Opik official site and BrightCoding review, 2026

stats.fact: Opik is trusted by over 150,000 developers worldwide
Opik GitHub stars grew from 0 to 12,500 in approximately 8-9 months, making it one of the fastest-growing projects in the LLM observability space
Opik's free hosted plan provides 25,000 spans per month with unlimited team members and 60-day data retention
Opik Pro plan costs $19/month for 100k spans, with additional 100k spans at $5
Langfuse has 19,000+ GitHub stars and is backed by ClickHouse after a $400M Series D acquisition in January 2026

angles: Fully open-source with no feature gating: unlike LangSmith (proprietary) or partial-open tools, Opik's full feature set is available for free self-hosting under Apache 2.0
Agent Optimizer as a key differentiator: 6 automated optimization algorithms that refine prompts and tool configurations — unique to Opik among open-source platforms
Unified ML + LLM workflow: teams already using Comet ML for experiment tracking get a seamless bridge to LLM observability without switching tools
Enterprise-grade scalability: 40M+ traces/day with Kubernetes Helm chart deployment makes it suitable for large-scale production workloads
Guardrails for responsible AI: built-in PII redaction, topic blocking, and safety checks — important for enterprise compliance
Growing rapidly: fastest-growing LLM observability project on GitHub in 2025, with Gartner recognition in 2026 validating enterprise credibility
LLM-as-a-judge made easy: built-in automated metrics for hallucination, toxicity, relevance, and RAG-specific metrics (Answer Relevance, Context Precision) out-of-the-box
CI/CD integration via PyTest: evaluation in the development pipeline is a critical shift for AI engineering teams adopting LLMOps
outline: H2: What Is Comet Opik? (Overview and Background)
H2: Core Features Deep Dive (Tracing, Evaluation, Agent Optimizer, Guardrails)
H2: Getting Started with Opik: Installation and Setup
H2: Integrations: Works With Your Entire LLM Stack
H2: Opik vs. LangSmith vs. Langfuse vs. Arize Phoenix (Head-to-Head)
H2: Pricing Breakdown: Free, Pro, and Enterprise
H2: Real-World Use Cases (RAG Applications, Agentic Workflows, Code Assistants)
H2: Self-Hosting Opik: Docker and Kubernetes Options
H2: Pros and Cons of Using Comet Opik
H2: Final Verdict: Who Should Use Opik in 2026?
